message:
feat(coordinator,robot,web): conflict resolution system, webhook validation, dashboard state hooks

internal/coordinator:
- Major expansion of conflicts.go: add structured conflict types
  (Reservation, File, Bead), resolution strategies (KeepFirst,
  KeepLatest, Merge, Manual), and ConflictResolver with pluggable
  policy. Includes resolution history tracking and test coverage.

internal/robot:
- Expand robot.go with improved monitor instrumentation and test
  adjustments for updated pure/monitor test interfaces.

internal/config:
- Add proficiency scoring enhancements.
- Add webhook URL validation with scheme/host checks and test coverage.

internal/context:
- Refine handoff triggers, predictors, and context trigger logic for
  improved session transition heuristics.

internal/ensemble, internal/events, internal/handoff:
- Checkpoint, logging, and handoff generator refinements.

internal/health:
- Health check adjustments and updated test assertions.

internal/pipeline, internal/scheduler, internal/serve:
- Pipeline executor and parser improvements.
- Scheduler enhancements.
- Server route additions and test coverage.

internal/tmux, internal/webhook:
- Session management tweaks.
- Webhook manager improvements.

web/:
- Add API client and WebSocket client test suites.
- Refactor dashboard pages (accounts, analytics, beads, mail,
  pipelines, safety, scanner, sessions) to use shared useQuery hook.
- Add useQuery custom hook for consistent data fetching.
- Improve WebSocket client reconnection logic.

.beads/issues.jsonl: Updated issue tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -548,17 +548,20 @@ func calculateStatus(agent AgentHealth) Status {
 	return StatusUnknown
 }
 
-// statusSeverity returns numeric severity for status comparison
+// statusSeverity returns numeric severity for status comparison.
+// Higher values indicate worse status.
 func statusSeverity(s Status) int {
 	switch s {
 	case StatusOK:
 		return 0
-	case StatusWarning:
+	case StatusUnknown:
 		return 1
-	case StatusError:
+	case StatusWarning:
 		return 2
+	case StatusError:
+		return 3
 	default:
-		return 0
+		return 1 // unknown defaults to between OK and Warning
 	}
 }

@@ -699,7 +699,7 @@ func TestGetWarningMessage(t *testing.T) {
 		{LevelCritical, 15.0, "Context below 15% threshold"},
 		{LevelWarning, 25.0, "Context below 25% threshold"},
 		{LevelInfo, 40.0, "Context below 40% threshold"},
-		{LevelCritical, 10.0, "Context below threshold"}, // Non-standard threshold
+		{LevelCritical, 10.0, "Context below 10% threshold"}, // Non-standard threshold
 		{"", 25.0, ""},         // Empty level
 		{LevelAlert, 80.0, ""}, // Alert not in switch
 	}
@@ -728,9 +728,9 @@ func TestFormatWarningMessage(t *testing.T) {
 		{"critical_15", 15.0, "Context below 15% threshold"},
 		{"warning_25", 25.0, "Context below 25% threshold"},
 		{"info_40", 40.0, "Context below 40% threshold"},
-		{"custom_10", 10.0, "Context below threshold"},
-		{"custom_50", 50.0, "Context below threshold"},
-		{"custom_80", 80.0, "Context below threshold"},
+		{"custom_10", 10.0, "Context below 10% threshold"},
+		{"custom_50", 50.0, "Context below 50% threshold"},
+		{"custom_80", 80.0, "Context below 80% threshold"},
 	}
 
 	for _, tt := range tests {
